title: verbascum × gaudinii
intro: Verbascum × gaudinii, also known as Gaudin's mullein, is a hybrid plant derived from the Verbascum thapsus species, commonly used in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Verbascum × gaudinii include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in the treatment of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to create topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
